commands.review_summary:
The branch adds poll-vote CLI/RPC/bridge support and a MessageStore poll-option lookup, and also changes poll send to emit the question as a separate text message before the poll.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for a feature request, but the merge blockers are source-reproducible: the diff sends an extra question message in handleSendPoll and omits option details from handlePollVote's response.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR changes native private-bridge message delivery and lacks inspectable live proof; contributor action is needed for proof and the two review findings.

target.title: feat(poll): cast votes on native Messages polls
target.body:
# feat(poll): cast votes on native Messages polls

## What & why

`imsg` could create and read native Apple Messages polls but not **vote** on them.
This adds `imsg poll vote`, plus the `poll.vote` RPC method and the `send-poll-vote`
bridge action, so a poll can be answered end to end.

## How it works

A vote is an *associated message* (`associatedMessageType 4000`, bare poll GUID)
that also carries the Polls balloon payload
(`{version:1,item:{votes:[{voteOptionIdentifier,participantHandle}]}}`).

The tricky part: **macOS 26 exposes no IMMessage initializer that carries a balloon
payload AND an associated message together** (verified by enumerating the class's
`init…` selectors). Reactions use the associated-message initializer; polls use the
balloon initializer; neither has both. So the vote is constructed with the
associated-message initializer (the path reactions use, which persists the
association atomically), and `balloonBundleID` / `payloadData` are then stamped onto
the message's own backing item — the one place they survive `[chat sendMessage:]`.

Option selection resolves a 1-based `--option-index`, an `--option` text
(case-insensitive), or a direct `--option-id` UUID to the stable
`optionIdentifier`, via a new `MessageStore.pollOptions(guid:)` that decodes the
originating poll. The resolved option label is echoed back in the result so callers
can suppress a redundant text reply that merely restates the vote.

## Surface added

- CLI: `imsg poll vote --chat <c> --poll <guid> (--option-index N | --option TEXT | --option-id UUID)`
- RPC: `poll.vote` / `messages.poll.vote`
- Bridge action: `send-poll-vote`
- `MessageStore.pollOptions(guid:)`

## Evidence

- `swift build` — Build complete; `make build-dylib` — built
- Live on macOS 26.4.1: votes by index, text, and UUID all land as
  `associated_message_type=4000` linked to the poll, `is_delivered=1`, and decode
  back as the expected option. Verified against the on-disk shape of native votes.

## Codex review (independent, gpt-5.4)

Reviewed to READY over 3 rounds:
- R1 NEEDS WORK: non-bare poll GUID forwarded as associatedMessageGUID; `--option-id` skipped poll-existence validation.
- R2: both prior resolved (barePollGuid on CLI+RPC; CLI validates); new — RPC path skipped the same validation.
- R3: **READY**, no findings — RPC now decodes the poll and rejects unknown poll / non-member option_id; the private-API "associated init then stamp balloon fields" construction is sound.
